references.text: Bernardi, L. 1964. Revisio generis Weinmannieae. III. Botaniche Jahrbucher 83: 126-184.
Bernardi, L. 1964. Revisio generis Weinmannieae. III. sect. III-VI (Veteris Orbis) Botaniche Jahrbucher 83: 185-221.
Hoogland, RD. 1979. Studies in the Cunoniaceae. II. The genera Caldcluvia, Pullea, Acsmithia, and Spiraeanthemum. Blumea 25: 481-505
Hoogland, RD. 2002. Cunoniaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 16: 51-165
Hopkins, HC. 1998. A revision of Weinmannia (Cunoniaceae) in Malesia and the Pacific. 2. Sulawesi and the Philippines. Adansonia 20: 43-66
Hopkins, HCF, JC Bradford & Y Pillon. 2023. An account of the Cunoniaceae in the Solomon archipelago and Vanuatu. Kew Bulletin 78: 433-467.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 25 January 2026

species.citation: (Blume) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1197; ≡ Spiraeopsis celebica (Blume) Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 1(1) (1856) 719 ; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 132; ≡ Caldcluvia celebica (Blume) Hoogland , Blumea 25 (1979) 485; ≡ Cunonia celebica Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 868; Type: from Sulawesi. = Spiraeopsis philippinensis Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2826 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 224; Type: Elmer 14157 (PNH, holo, lost; L, lecto; A, B, BISH, BM, FI, G, GH, K, MO, NSW, NY, U, US, W, Z, isolecto). = Spiraeopsis luzonensis Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3810.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. CAMIGUIN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon (photos), Davao de Oro (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Misamis Oriental (photos), NEGROS.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 February 2025

species.citation: (Merr.) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198; ≡ Weinmannia hutchinsonii Merr. , PJS 2c (1907) 275 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 224; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 157; Coritico et al., J. Trop. Biol. & Cons. 19 (2022) 148; Type: Merrill 5753 (FI, K, NY, P, US), Mindoro: Mt Ha1con. = Weinmannia camiguinensis Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2607 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 224; Type: Elmer 14228 (A, BM, BO, GH, K, L, P, PNH, and others), Mindanao: Camiguin, Mambajao. = Weinmannia bulusanensis Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3723, nom. inval. = Weinmannia irosinensis Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3725, nom. inval.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CAMIGUIN, CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), JOLO, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Camarines Norte, Pampanga, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY (photos).

species.citation: (Merr.) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198; ≡ Weinmannia lucida Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 7 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 225; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 158; Type: Ramos, Philipp. Pl. 1109 (FI, G, JE, M, U, US, Z), Luzon: Laguna, Dahican.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Laguna, Rizal, SAMAR (photos).

species.citation: (S.Vidal) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198; ≡ Weinmannia luzoniensis S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 125 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 225; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 158; Type: Vidal 314 (MA, lecto; P, A, FI, L, MA, isolecto), Luzon: Tayabas, Lucban. = Weinmannia luzoniensis var. puberula Elmer , LPB 8 (1919) 3078; Type: Elmer 18066 (A, BM, BO, K, L, P, PNH and others), Luzon: Laguna, Los Banos, Mt Makiling.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BATANES, CAMIGUIN, CULION, LUZON: Abra, Apayao (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, SAMAR.

species.citation: (Elmer) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198; ≡ Weinmannia negrosensis Elmer , LPB 2 (1909) 577 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 225; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 159; Type: Elmer 9656 (A, BM, E, FI, G, LE, Z), Negros Oriental: Dumaguete, Cuernos Mts. = Weinmannia negrosensis var. simplicifolia (Merr.) Bernardi , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 83 (1964) 175; ≡ Weinmannia simplicifolia Merr. , PJS 12c (1917) 268 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 225; Type: Ramos & Edano BS 26531 (A, K), Luzon: Tayabas, Mt Dingalan. = Weinmannia cuneatifolia Engl. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 18a (1928) 252; Type: Ramos Philipp. Pl. 1287 (B, holo; BM, L, P and others, iso), Mindanao.
species.distribution: Philippines, Sulawesi. CAMIGUIN, LEYTE, LUZON: Aurora (photos), Bataan, Mountain Province, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, NEGROS, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Very closely allied to Weinmannia luzoniensis S.Vidal. Forests. 300-1950m.

species.citation: (Elmer) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198; ≡ Weinmannia urdanetensis Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2608 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 225; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 161; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 108; Type: Elmer 13701 (A, BISH, BM, BO, K, L and others), Mindanao: Agusan, Cabadbaran (Mt Urdaneta).
species.distribution: New Guinea, Philippines. DINAGAT, LUZON: Ilocos Norte, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon (photos), Davao Oriental.

species.citation: D.Don , Edinburgh New Philos. J. 9 (1830) 93. = Weinmannia blumei Planch. , London J. Bot. 6 (1847) 470.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, ?Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sumatra, Thailand. ?LUZON: ?Albay.
species.notes: Pelser (31-Jul-22): Reynoso, Sagcal & Fernando PPI 21347 (L) from Mt Malinao possibly represents this species in the Philippines.

species.citation: (H.C.Hopkins) Pillon & H.C.Hopkins , Amer. J. Bot. 108 (2021) 1198. = Weinmannia furfuracea H.C.Hopkins , Adansonia, 3rd Sér. 10 (1998) 49 ; Fortune Hopkins & Hoogland, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 155.
species.notes: Possibly represented in the Philippines by Ingle 136 (K, PNH). 1700-2000m.
